sections.content: The LEFT action changed the board, but the animation bbox is at [61, 23, 62, 23] which is at the bottom of the screen - likely the timer/progress bar. The blocks themselves might not have moved, or they moved but the change was minimal. Let me check the block positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check block positions after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'b'] and n['id'] in [6, 7]:
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check the area around blocks
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(14, 21):
    print(f"Row {r}, cols 30-42: '{ascii_grid[r][30:42]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blocks didn't move! They're still at the same position (orange at rows 15-16, cols 34-38; blue at rows 17-19, cols 34-38). The LEFT action only changed the timer/progress bar at the bottom.
So the blocks are stuck at the top. They can't move UP (blocked by black box), and LEFT/RIGHT don't seem to move them either (or they're blocked horizontally too).
Wait, but earlier LEFT/RIGHT did move the blocks when they were at the bottom of the room. Let me check if the blocks are now constrained because they're in a narrower passage.
Looking at the ASCII:
- Row 15: ccGBOOOOOBGc - the orange block is between G cells on both sides
- Row 17: ccccbbbbbccc - the blue block is between c cells on both sides
So the blocks are in a narrow passage and can't move LEFT or RIGHT because they're blocked by the walls.
Let me try RIGHT to confirm, and also try DOWN to see if the blocks can move back down.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try RIGHT to see if blocks move
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
